Now, here are these two major points I want everyone to focus on. Number one, I left these out of the training because I wanted to focus on simply while you're actually writing. There's two little hacks that I use when I write scripts that work incredibly well for the main body. Number one, whenever possible, I want you to link your good or service to a trustworthy or celebrity or known figure. When you can do that, it has a massive impact on creating certain, like for instance, you're in the financial world, like Warren Buffett believes, said the same thing. Don't lie, by the way, I'm just giving you an example. If any authority figure well-known has made a statement or endorses a similar product, use your truth well-told strategy and link that to your product without lying. Do not lie. Link it to your product. In addition, try as you're talking about these features and benefits. I want you to try to weave them into a story. Don't just make everything dry. A feature and a benefit could be, it has this feature, in fact, let me tell you a story. Very well-planned doors, I'll say, it has this thing which is crucial because it does X, Y, Z. In fact, let me tell you a story about a client I had. I tell you a story about a client. We've been conditioned to really understand things through the use of stories. Goes back to the caveman days. We are conditioned to learn through storytelling. So try to use stories, paint a picture. Don't just make it very dry, feature, benefit, feature, benefit. So look at your top six features, top five action, but you can make it longer if you want that. There's no rule for this, but this evolution.
